title: Cherry-Pick, Replay, Patch Equivalence, and Backports · Conflict Triage
summary: A responder inherits a plausible but defective multi-line backport and patch-equivalence record; preserve reachable history, isolate the first causal Git-state mismatch, and retain a regression probe. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in multi-line backport and patch-equivalence record and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Select and order commits for cherry-pick using explicit revision traversal while distinguishing argument order from revision-walk order.
Handle merge mainline, empty, redundant, no-commit, signoff, and conflict continuation choices with release-policy evidence.
Use patch-id, cherry marks, range-diff, and tree comparisons to detect likely equivalent changes while naming heuristic limits.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:
debug case — Case G34 is a debug in a disposable repository. one differently named backport is patch-equivalent and should not be replayed twice. The learner must predict minus marks likely equivalent patches while plus marks changes absent from the target, run git cherry -v release security-fix, explain the parser fix is equivalent but the dependency-bound test remains target-specific work, test this changed condition: alter context without changing the logical patch and compare heuristic patch IDs with behavior, and preserve this recovery boundary: tag each target pre-backport tip under refs/recovery before sequencer operations. Competency probe: Select and order commits for cherry-pick using explicit revision traversal while distinguishing argument order from revision-walk order.

Inspect the Gita Cherry Pick Set Order trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-backport-verification-ta1i0w" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Backport Verification into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-backport-verification-ta1i0w
# scenario-key: trace-ta1i0w probe-w0i1at boundary-1i0wta owner-ta1i0wx fault-ta1i0wz
# before-ref: main=a11ceta candidate=b00b10w
# hypothesis: one differently named backport is patch-equivalent and should not be replayed twice
git cherry -v release security-fix
# expected: minus marks likely equivalent patches while plus marks changes absent from the target
# observation: the parser fix is equivalent but the dependency-bound test remains target-specific work
# changed-case: alter context without changing the logical patch and compare heuristic patch IDs with behavior
# after-ref: main=a11ceta candidate=c0ffeew
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: tag each target pre-backport tip under refs/recovery before sequencer operations
# transfer-limit: patch equivalence is heuristic and each supported runtime needs changed-platform tests
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
